verdict: 2026-06-27 18:21:46 PDT — Spec-first, I would keep the two-tier split: roadmap as sequencer, backlog as detail store, with dated current-state reconciliation and linked execution rows. The chosen shape is directionally right, but it is not yet cleanly DRY or internally reconciled.

findings.title: Reconciliation leaves stale shipped/removed claims in the roadmap
findings.claim: The refreshed table says data delete/export backend shipped and the multiplier was removed, but later roadmap sections still say Phase 1 data/API changes are to add delete/export endpoints, say delete/export endpoints are still required, and keep `multiplier` in the proposed future `settings` model. That makes the strategic doc contradict its own reconciled current-state claims.
findings.alternative: Sweep the roadmap sections that remain authoritative after the Current State table: rewrite Phase 1 data/API changes as UI/privacy-page/deauth follow-up only, update the privacy cross-cutting note to say backend endpoints shipped and remaining work is UI/policy/deauth, and remove `multiplier` from the proposed split model except as a legacy ignored field if needed.
findings.win: Eliminates false future work and prevents a later implementation story from resurrecting a removed cost-model field or rebuilding endpoints that already shipped.

findings.title: Execution plan duplicates backlog detail without stable item links
findings.claim: The Execution plan says per-item detail lives in the backlog, but most backlog-derived rows copy tactical descriptions and are not linked to the corresponding backlog headings. That weakens the approved two-tier split and leaves two prose stores to keep synchronized.
findings.alternative: Make each backlog-derived Item cell a linked backlog heading or review file, keep only minimal sequencing metadata in the roadmap, and move detailed what/why text back to `reviews/backlog.md`. Add an explicit exclusion row for any non-project backlog heading that is intentionally omitted.
findings.win: Restores one detail authority, gives direct navigation both ways, and removes duplicated clauses that can drift from the backlog.

findings.title: Dependency bump is still sequenced as open despite the manifest showing it done
findings.claim: The new authoritative plan lists `esbuild / Vite dev-only bump` as open work, and the backlog still describes the done state as reconciling to Vite 6.4.2 plus esbuild >=0.25. The manifest already has `client` on `vite` `^6.4.2`, the lock resolves direct `vite` to 6.4.3, and `esbuild` to 0.25.12, so the plan is carrying a phantom open item unless a different dependency task remains.
findings.alternative: Either mark the backlog item DONE and remove it from the Execution plan, or rename it to the actual remaining dependency concern with evidence from the manifest/lockfile.
findings.win: Keeps the roadmap’s open-work inventory truthful and avoids spending a future story on an already-satisfied dependency bump.
